notes: L9 'Part 3 — permute + batch (443.0s, 3 cues)' — Code It From Scratch: PIL/NumPy preprocessing to (1,3,896,896) float32 for Gemma 4 vision. Reuses imgprep.py idiom lib. READ start/mid/end each cue; all legible, in-frame (safe-area guard passed), on-brand (dark palette, blue/orange/green, mono code + serif prose). Math verified: ImageNet mean/std exact, shape transitions (H,W,C)->(C,H,W)->(1,C,H,W) correct, float64 trap correct. ITER2: final spot-check caught Cue1 mid-window collision (gray permute-axes label overlapping blue channels-FIRST caption, visible beats ~70-96 / t175-243s in final; -ql end-frame review had missed it because the label fades by cue end). Fixed activity_62.py:136 (moved permute label ABOVE chips, move_to(UP*1.15)); re-rendered Cue1 -ql + READ beat-80 mid frame = clean, both labels clear, no overlap. Re-rendering final Cue1. ffprobe within 0.13s of storyboard span.
